neck thy run, now this one forging
ahead and then another. The motley
crowd of braves lining the sides of
the course whoop to encourage their
favoriteS. Bets are made, not none
tary, but for the possible exchange
of horses or of skins or Of beads.
The finish is the occasion for horrify-
ing and ear-splitting whoops The
victor is carried upon the shoulders
of his enthusiastic admirers and a
general good time ends the sport. To
the person who never has seen Indian
sports of any kind. this will prove ex-
ceptionally interesting.


Besides the pony races, there are
races afoot and endurance races be-
tween man and horse. In the latter,
a man races afoot against horse and
rider. and in many instances he has
been known to beat his opponents
In addition to the athletics on In
dian day, the war dances of the tribes
will be given. Among the tribes par
ticipating will be the Selitz, klamath,
Yakima, Umatilla, Puyallup and Chi
nook and student athletes from the
Chemawa, Paris, Haskell and Carlisle
Indian schools.
